name: iDCOL
shortDescription: Implicit differentiable collision detection for strictly convex contact geometry.
kicker: Analytical contact kinematics for gradient-based robotics.
description: iDCOL is a differentiable contact kinematic framework for strictly convex contact geometry, designed for gradient-based simulation, planning, and optimization in contact-rich robotic systems.
body: At its core, iDCOL reduces contact computation to a fixed-size nonlinear solve, making it fast, differentiable, and easy to integrate into physics engines and optimizers.
features: Robust collision detection for strictly convex implicit shapes
A lightweight C++ API suitable for physics engines and optimizers

name: Implicit Routing
shortDescription: Differentiable simulation of soft continuum manipulators with implicit internal cable routing.
kicker: Let the cable path emerge from equilibrium.
description: Built on the SoRoSim toolbox, Implicit Routing simulates soft continuum manipulators whose internal cable paths are solved implicitly rather than prescribed.
body: Given cable entry and exit ports, the routing is solved as part of the manipulator's static equilibrium. The complete pipeline is differentiable end-to-end, with analytical Jacobians throughout.
features: Implicit cable routing through deformable manipulators
Static equilibrium coupling between robot shape and cable path
Built on the SoRoSim soft robotics simulator
